food_subgroup: Processed poultry — breaded and baked chicken strips

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03302051 represents chicken tender food product as a closest parent; no exact code for oven-baked breaded chicken tenders exists in the current release.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP (Chicken meat, cooked) is the closest defensible parent; no specific code for breaded oven-baked chicken tenders exists in FoodEx2 release 2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226529007 represents chicken strip food product as closest parent; exact oven-baked breaded variant not separately coded.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is 6 pieces of oven-baked crispy chicken tenders, approximately 210 g total, as served by Now Pizza - Funan on field survey data.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken breast tenders, cooked, roasted — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 05298)
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for cooked chicken breast tenders (roasted). The restaurant product is described as oven-baked with a crispy breadcrumb coating; macros have been adjusted upward for carbohydrate and fat to account for the breaded coating, using USDA Branded Food reference for breaded baked chicken tenders (FDC ID 2103697) as a secondary cross-check. Energy recalculated from adjusted macros using Atwater factors.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.organization: American Journal of Clinical Nutrition / University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for oven-baked breaded chicken tenders. GI of 45 is estimated by analogy with breaded and baked chicken products (GI ~40–50) as reported in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables. The dominant carbohydrate source is the wheat-based breadcrumb coating; GI of white bread coating is moderated by the protein and fat matrix, yielding an estimated composite GI of 45 (low). Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (45 × 26.9) / 100 ≈ 12.1, where carbohydrate per 210 g serving = 12.8 × 2.1 = 26.9 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~480 mg per 100 g; ~1008 mg per 6-piece serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to 2–3 pieces and avoid high-sodium dipping sauces such as the accompanying Hot Sauce or BBQ dip, as the combined sodium load from a full 6-piece serving with dips may approach or exceed 1200 mg per meal.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (~12 per serving) from wheat-based breadcrumb coating
type2_diabetes.guidance: The breaded coating contributes approximately 26.9 g of carbohydrate per 6-piece serving with an estimated GI of 45; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should account for this carbohydrate load within their meal plan and pair with non-starchy vegetables rather than high-GI sides to moderate postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~2.1 g per 100 g; ~4.4 g per serving) and sodium load
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: While oven-baking reduces fat compared to deep-frying, the breaded coating and seasoning contribute moderate saturated fat and high sodium per serving; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit to 3 pieces per occasion and choose the lower-sodium dip option or omit dips entirely.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chicken Tenders (6 pieces, oven-baked) is a Western fast-casual dish consisting of six strips of chicken breast tenderloin coated in a seasoned wheat-based breadcrumb crust and cooked in an oven rather than deep-fried, resulting in a crispy exterior with a juicy, tender interior. The dish is served by Now Pizza - Funan in Singapore via field survey and is typically paired with dipping sauces such as Hot Sauce or Smokey BBQ. Nutritionally, each 100 g provides approximately 218 kcal, 20.5 g protein, 9.2 g fat, and 12.8 g carbohydrate, making it a moderate-calorie, high-protein option. The oven-baking method reduces total fat compared to deep-fried equivalents, though the breadcrumb coating adds carbohydrate and some sodium (~480 mg per 100 g). The estimated glycemic index is 45 (low), with a glycemic load of approximately 12 per 6-piece serving. Clinically, the sodium content warrants attention for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease, and the carbohydrate contribution from the coating should be factored into meal planning for those with Type 2 Diabetes. The dish is not suitable for gluten-free diets due to the wheat-based coating. It is a good source of lean protein, niacin, vitamin B6, phosphorus, and selenium.
common_user_queries: How many calories in 6 chicken tenders?
Now Pizza Funan chicken tenders calories
Is chicken tender good for diabetics?

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry exists for this specific restaurant product in any approved primary source. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for cooked chicken breast tenders and cross-checked against USDA Branded Food entries for breaded baked chicken tenders. The GI is an analogous estimate. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 05298 / FDC 171477) for cooked chicken breast tenders, with carbohydrate and fat adjusted upward to account for the wheat-based breadcrumb coating, cross-referenced against USDA Branded Food FDC ID 2103697. 2. Vitamin A (vitamin_a_mcg_rae) is -1 as it is not consistently reported across the reference entries used and cannot be reliably estimated for this product. 3. Folate (folate_mcg_dfe) is -1 as it is not reported in the primary USDA SR Legacy entry for this food category. 4. Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this product. 5. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this product. 6. Vitamin C (vitamin_c_mg) is 0.0 — confirmed zero; chicken breast tenders contain negligible vitamin C per USDA FoodData Central. 7. GI of 45 is an analogous estimate based on breaded baked chicken products in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables; no direct measurement exists for this specific product. 8. Halal status is listed as false as it cannot be confirmed from available public information for Now Pizza - Funan; consumers requiring halal-certified food should verify directly with the restaurant. 9. Serving size of 210 g is estimated based on typical 6-piece chicken tender portions (approximately 35 g per piece); actual weight may vary by restaurant. 10. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are estimated from USDA FoodData Central reference data for comparable cooked breaded chicken products.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Built complete record from scratch. Applied USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy as primary nutrient source with adaptation for breaded coating. Applied analogous GI estimate from University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2021. Added multilingual names in Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi for Singapore context. Documented all -1 sentinel values in notes. Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases consistency. Confirmed gi_category 'low' for GI 45 per ≤55 threshold rule. Confirmed all ontology match_type values within allowed set. Confirmed no zero core macros without justification. Self-scored 8/8 with all hard gates passing. Decision: PASS on iteration 1.
